    Home › Aircraft › Boeing C-17 Globemaster III

    Boeing C-17 Globemaster III

    Boeing · Military transport · In service worldwide

    The West's do-everything airlifter.

    Length53.04 m
    Wingspan51.75 m
    Range8,700 km
    MTOW265 t
    Built279
    First flight1991
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Range per tonne#30 of 40
    32.8 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#12 of 43
    0.98 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Boeing C-17 Globemaster III's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Boeing C-17 Globemaster III51.75 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #32 of 43 by length → #14 by weight → #38 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length53.04 m
    Wingspan51.75 m
    Height16.80 m
    MTOW265,352 kg
    Max payload77,519 kg
    Range (with 72 t)≈8,700 km
    Engines4 × PW F117 (PW2000)
    First flight15 September 1991
    Built279 (line closed 2015)

    Compare it

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The C-17's genius is contradiction: it hauls strategic loads — a 60-tonne main battle tank — yet lands on 1,000-metre dirt strips, backs up under its own reverse thrust, and turns around on a runway the width of a road. Nothing else combines intercontinental reach with rough-field manners at this scale.

    It became the machine the world reaches for when everything goes wrong: earthquake relief, evacuation airlifts, aid drops. The 2021 Kabul evacuation made one C-17 famous for carrying 823 people in a single lift.

    279 were built before the line closed in 2015 — a decision the US has publicly regretted since, as no replacement exists. Nine air forces fly it, and every operator's answer to 'what would you buy more of?' is the same.

    Variants

    The Boeing C-17 Globemaster III line

    C-17A
    The single production standard — continually upgraded.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
